name: São Tomé and Príncipe (Democratic Republic of São Tomé and Príncipe / República Democrática de São Tomé e Príncipe)
summary: São Tomé and Príncipe is an Atlantic African civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Código Civil) drawing on Portuguese civil-law substantive heritage, supplemented by the Family Code (Lei N.º 2/77 with subsequent amendments), the Law on the Promotion and Protection of Children 2006 (Law 2/2006), and Law 11/2007 on Filiation. Parental responsibility (responsabilidade parental) and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 1875-1910 and Law on Children's Protection 2006. The Supreme Court of Justice (Supremo Tribunal de Justiça) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Primeira Instância). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. São Tomé and Príncipe is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard. São Tomé and Príncipe is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.citation: Family Code Law 2/77 (with subsequent amendments) arts. 1875-1910
statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental responsibility and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on Portuguese civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 1875-1910 govern responsabilidade parental and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Law on Promotion and Protection of Children 2006 (Law 2/2006)
statutory_framework.title: Law on Promotion and Protection of Children
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on Children's Protection aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Supremo Tribunal de Justiça)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, São Tomé and Príncipe
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: São Toméan family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: São Tomé and Príncipe independence + Manuel Pinto da Costa + MLSTP framework
key_developments.description: São Tomé and Príncipe independence 12 July 1975 from Portugal following Carnation Revolution 25 April 1974 — Manuel Pinto da Costa first president substantively distinctive Lusophone African Marxist-Leninist framework. Substantive MLSTP (Movement for the Liberation of São Tomé and Príncipe) framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Family Code Law 2/77
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted Law 2/77 of 7 February 1977 drawing on Portuguese civil-law substantive heritage. Arts. 1875-1910 govern responsabilidade parental and child custody. Foundational substantive-family-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution + multi-party-democratic transition framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of São Tomé and Príncipe 1990 substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing multi-party-democratic framework. Substantively significant Lusophone African democratic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: São Tomé and Príncipe ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: São Tomé and Príncipe ratified the UNCRC on 14 May 1991 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2006 Law on Promotion and Protection of Children substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Príncipe Regional Autonomy 1995 + autonomous-region framework
key_developments.description: Príncipe Regional Autonomy established 29 April 1995 — substantively distinctive Lusophone Atlantic island-state autonomous-region framework. Substantive constitutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 2003 attempted coup + recurring-political-instability framework
key_developments.description: 2003 attempted coup 16 July 2003 — substantively distinctive Lusophone African recurring-political-instability framework. Substantive political-institutional-disruption framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Law on Promotion and Protection of Children (Law 2/2006) + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Law on Promotion and Protection of Children Law 2/2006 of 28 June 2006 aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Lusophone African framework.

key_developments.title: Law 11/2007 on Filiation + family-law-statutory framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Law 11/2007 on Filiation enacted substantively reforming filiation framework. Substantively significant family-law-modernisation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: CPLP framework + Lusophone-cooperation + diaspora trajectory
key_developments.description: CPLP (Community of Portuguese-Speaking Countries) framework consolidation through 2010s-2020s substantively affecting Lusophone cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with Portugal, Brazil, Angola, Mozambique, Cabo Verde, Guinea-Bissau, East Timor. Substantial diaspora in Portugal substantively affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework.

key_developments.title: INPG framework + Provedor de Justiça + CPLP institutional consolidation
key_developments.description: Instituto Nacional para a Promoção da Igualdade e Equidade do Género (INPG) and Provedor de Justiça (Ombudsperson) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Family Code Law 2/77 + Law 11/2007 framework. CPLP institutional consolidation framework substantively significant for Lusophone cross-border family-law adjudication. Substantively significant Equatorial Africa Lusophone institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supremo Tribunal de Justiça + Tribunal Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Carlos Vila Nova presidency
key_developments.description: Supremo Tribunal de Justiça and Tribunal Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code Law 2/77 arts. 1875-1910 + Law 2/2006 + Law 11/2007 framework in custody disputes within Carlos Vila Nova (Independent Democratic Action) presidency since 2 October 2021. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: São Tomé and Príncipe operates a Portuguese-civil-law family-law framework — places São Tomé and Príncipe in the Lusophone African cluster with Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Angola, Portugal, Brazil, East Timor within the corpus.
Atlantic island state status with Lusophone heritage is structurally distinctive.
Non-Hague Convention status places São Tomé and Príncipe in the non-Hague Lusophone cluster alongside Angola.

sources.title: Courts of São Tomé and Príncipe

editorial_notes: São Tomé and Príncipe jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Pinto-da-Costa-to-Vila-Nova trajectory: 1975-São-Tomé-and-Príncipe-independence-+-Pinto-da-Costa-+-MLSTP + 1977-Family-Code-Law-2-77 + 1990-Constitution-+-multi-party-democratic + 1991-UNCRC-ratification + 1995-Príncipe-Regional-Autonomy + 2003-2003-attempted-coup + 2006-Law-on-Promotion-and-Protection-of-Children-2-2006 + 2007-Law-11-2007-on-Filiation + 2018-CPLP-framework-+-Lusophone-cooperation-+-diaspora + 2024-Supremo-Tribunal-de-Justiça-+-Vila-Nova-presidency.

Civil-law Lusophone Atlantic island (Family Code Law 2/77 + Law on Promotion and Protection of Children 2006 + non-Hague Convention). Completes Lusophone African cluster (Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Angola, São Tomé).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code Law 2/77 arts. 1875-1910 + Law 2/2006 + Law 11/2007 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Atlantic-African + Lusophone (with Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Angola, Portugal, Brazil, East Timor, Guinea-Bissau) + civil-law + Atlantic-island-state-Lusophone-distinctive + MLSTP-Marxist-Leninist-1975-1990 + Príncipe-Regional-Autonomy-1995-distinctive + 2003-attempted-coup-recurring-political-instability + CPLP-Lusophone-framework + substantial-diaspora-Portugal + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
